title: neonauclea bartlingii var. bartlingii
intro: Neonauclea bartlingii var. bartlingii, also known as Bunga Kelapa or Coconut Bunga, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, particularly in the Philippines and Indonesia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Neonauclea bartlingii var. bartlingii include reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and soothing skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, and it is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
